violations.description: Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food prepared and packaged by a processing plant was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days after original package was opened. Two boxes of macaroni salad were found in the walk-in cooler past the use by date on the boxes. Expired products should not be used by licensed kitchens. These should be discarded. Portioned cups of salads in the refrigerated prep table are not date marked. Date marking should be done to assure these salads are disposed properly if they are not used with-in 10 days of removal from the package, or the expiration date, if closer.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/food/ice in unpackaged form was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Nozzles on the beverage dispenser were found dirty. Nozzles should be cleaned and maintained clean.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Vents for the pizza ovens were found dirty. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. To-go boxes were found stored on the floor behind the reach-in freezer in the kitchen. Single use item should be stored off of the floor.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --Employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. Employee beverage was found stored on the refrigerated prep table where portion cups of ranch were being prepared. Personal items should be stored separated from items used in food service.
Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Commercially prepared bucket of potato salad was found expired int he walk-in cooler. Expired items should not be used.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/food/ice in unpackaged form was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Nozzles on the beverage dispenser were found dirty. Nozzles should be cleaned and maintained clean.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Doors for the Blue Bunny chest freezer were found dirty. Reach in cooler where chicken is stored also was found dirty These units should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Employee observed washing hands in a warewash sink. Observed food employee washing hands in the 3 compartment sink. Hands should be washed in the designated hand sink.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
